title: The money market: foundational concepts
description: Practice concepts related to the money market, liquidity preference theory, and nominal interest rate adjustment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios will cause an increase in the demand for money in the money market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The consumer price index (CPI) increases.
The unemployment rate increases
Nominal gross domestic product (GDP) decreases
National income decreases
The demand for bonds increases

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following leads to a decrease in the demand for money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A lower price level
People want to hold more money.
Nominal gross domestic product (GDP) increases
Output increases
The demand for bonds decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following terms describes why households hold money to buy things?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: transactions motive
precautionary motive
speculative demand
the demand for loanable funds
excess reserves

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the relationship shown by the demand for money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A tradeoff exists between earning interest and the transactions motive
Money becomes less liquid as interest rates increase
Banks are more willing to create money when interest rates are high
People are more willing to hold money when the opportunity cost of holding money increases
When the price level increases, the amount of money people want to hold increases

assessment_items.question_content:
**Liquidity preference theory is used to motivate**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the demand for money.
the supply of money.
the demand for loanable funds.
the production possibilities curve.
the aggregate demand curve.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true when the money market is in equilibrium?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No economic forces are acting on the economy to change the interest rate.
People hold more money than desired at the current interest rate.
People hold less money than desired at the current interest rate.
The interest rate is decreasing.
The interest rate is increasing.

assessment_items.question_content:
The amount of money that people want to hold is greater than the money supply at the current interest rate.

**Which of the following describes the process that returns the money market to equilibrium when the quantity of money demanded is greater than the money supply?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People sell their bonds, which lowers bond prices, which in turn increases interest rates.
People buy bonds, which lowers bond prices, which in turn increases interest rates.
People buy bonds, which increases bond prices, which in turn lowers interest rates.
People sell their bonds, which lowers bond prices, which in turn decreases interest rates.
People sell their bonds, which raises bond prices, which in turn decreases interest rates.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes an equilibrium in the money market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The nominal interest rate adjusted until people are holding the money they want to hold
The real interest rate adjusted until people are holding the money they want to hold
The nominal interest rate has adjusted until people are holding more money than they want to hold
The real interest rate has adjusted until people are holding more money than they want to hold
Nominal interest rates are not changing and people are holding less money than they would like to hold

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes why the money supply curve is vertical?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Central banks determine the monetary base independent of the interest rate
Individuals hold less money when interest rates increase
The central bank responds to increases in the interest rate by increasing the quantity of money supplied
The central bank sets interest rates and changes the quantity of money accordingly
Savers want to supply more money when interest rates are high

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following graphs shows the correct relationship between the interest rate and the money supply when a country’s monetary base is determined by a central bank?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A Cartesian coordinate system with a vertical axis labeled "nom. int" and a horizontal axis labeled "Q M." An upward-sloping straight line labeled "S M" intersects the horizontal axis at two points marked "q M1" and "q M2." Two horizontal dashed lines extend from the upward slope to the vertical axis, intersecting it at points labeled "n ir1" and "n ir2." The dashed lines indicate the values on the vertical axis corresponding to "q M1" and "q M2." The structure is framed with a dashed red rectangle, enclosing the entire graph.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9ac9beec19a1df89bf4fdc18b5350cf622d5e378)

![A two-dimensional graph with a vertical axis labeled "real interest" and a horizontal axis labeled "quantity of labor force," featuring a straight, upward-sloping line representing "S L F." The line intersects two horizontal dashed lines labeled "r i r one" and "r i r two," indicating two distinct levels of real interest. The horizontal lines extend to the vertical axis at points labeled "q L F one" and "q L F two," marking specific quantities on the horizontal axis. The overall structure shows relationships between the quantity of labor force and real interest.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23b887705b4989817ddbea1ccb4f2873193630fd)

![A graph with a downward-sloping line labeled S M, indicating supply, intersects the horizontal axis labeled Q M at two distinct points marked q M, representing quantities. Two dashed vertical lines extend from the S M line to the horizontal axis, located to the left of the point q M and labeled n i r 1 and n i r 2, indicating two different price levels. The vertical axis is labeled nom. int. and does not display specific numerical values. The graph does not include any additional decorations or visual elements.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/569e31d43d1b5aba2b609495fcf5bf33c43012f4)

![A two-dimensional graph with a vertical axis labeled "nominal interest rate" and a horizontal axis labeled "quantity of money." The horizontal line representing the nominal interest rate is at a fixed height, intersecting the vertical axis at a point labeled "nir." The curve labeled "S M" is drawn horizontally from the left side to the right side, passing through two vertical dashed lines extending down to points labeled "q M1" and "q M2" on the horizontal axis, indicating specific quantities of money.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/135401cb2cb39bafd1ab90cc589707f4c6914609)

![A two-dimensional graph with the vertical axis labeled as "nominal interest" and the horizontal axis labeled as "quantity M." The line representing quantity M is horizontal, positioned at a height corresponding to "S M." Two dashed horizontal lines labeled "n i r 1" and "n i r 2" are drawn parallel to the horizontal axis, indicating specific values below the "S M" line. The graph does not feature any curves or additional elements, and the scale of the axes is not specified.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4a9bac3b8541c437c694883c3dd979f878938f5b)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how the money supply responds to changes in the nominal interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The money supply is independent of the interest rate.
The quantity of money supplied increases as the interest rate increases.
The quantity of money supplied decreases as the interest rate increases.
The quantity of money increases as the interest rate increases up to a point, and then decreases.
The quantity of money decreases as the interest rate increases up to a point, and then increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to the nominal interest rate and the quantity of money in the money supply if the demand for money increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nominal interest rate increases; no change in the quantity of money.
Nominal interest rate decreases; no change in the quantity of money.
Nominal interest rate increases; the quantity of money decreases.
Nominal interest rate decreases; the quantity of money increases.
Nominal interest rate increases; the quantity of money increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the money market, if nominal interest rates have increased and the quantity of money decreased, which of the following could have caused this change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The money supply decreased.
The money supply increased.
The demand for money decreased.
The demand for money increased.
The money supply increased and the money demand decreased.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nominal interest rates increased in Hamsterville, and there is less money available. 

**Which of the following could have caused this change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a decrease in the money supply
an increase in the money supply
a decrease in the demand for money
an increase in the demand for money
an increase in the demand for money and a decrease in the supply of money.

assessment_items.question_content:
The nominal interest rate in Maxistan decreased, but there is no change in people's liquidity preferences. 

**Which of the following could have caused this change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an increase in the money supply
a decrease in the money supply
an increase in the demand for money
a decrease in the demand for money
an increase in the demand for money and a decrease in the supply of money.

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that people keep all money in banks.

**What happens if banks decide to start keeping excess reserves instead of fully loaning out?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The money supply decreases.
The money supply increases.
The demand for money increases.
The demand for money decreases.
Aggregate demand increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
If the reserve requirement in a banking system is lowered, what is the most likely consequence?

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The nominal interest rate decreases.
The nominal interest rate increases.
The demand for money increases.
The demand for money decreases.
The demand for money increases and the supply of money decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If banks decide to keep fewer excess reserves and instead lend more, which of the following is the most likely effect?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The nominal interest rate decreases.
The nominal interest rate increases.
The demand for money increases.
The demand for money decreases.
The demand for money increases and the supply of money decreases.
